body.title: /decided skill — batch-ticketize design conclusions
body.result: ~/.claude/skills/decided/SKILL.md — scopes to last /design or prior /decided; summarizes, drafts tickets, runs /review per draft (HIGH-inertia asks inline), files batch w/ decision_id linkage, writes to palace + decisions log + slate + session. Two-way navigation via decision_id + spawned_tickets. 5dfd5c34.
body.description:
Closes the gap where decisions dictated in conversation get lost before being ticketed.

## Flow
1. Take last N conversation turns since (a) prior /decided, (b) /design marker, or (c) session start — whichever is most recent
2. Summarize the decision (1-2 sentences) — assign D### id
3. Draft tickets needed to implement it
4. Call /review on each drafted ticket (see T-review-as-filing-time-skill)
5. Amendment / split / discard based on /review findings
6. Write outputs:
   - queue.json: new tickets with decision_id=D### (see T-ticket-schema-decision-id-and-gate)
   - Igor memory palace: new node at theigors/decisions/D### with spawned_tickets=[...]
   - decisions log: simple chronological append (TBD which file — confirm with Akien, memory says don't write to decisions_log.dsb)
   - today's slate ## Ad hoc: decision + ticket ids
   - session record: session_manager.py append-decision D###

## Invocable standalone
No /design prereq — /decided infers scope from recent conversation.

## Not in scope
- Decision rollup (separate ticket)
- /design as explicit marker (separate ticket, optional)
